reviews.text: Excelente la comida y la atención de su equipo, para un restaurante de hotel, altamente recomendado.
reviews.author: Isaias Arancibia

reviews.text: Nice!
reviews.author: Estefaníacrv

reviews.text: Really fresh sushi.
reviews.author: Francis Asare

reviews.text: Yu is a great find. The service is personal, and the food is fresh and delicious—albeit on the pricey side. The décor strikes a balance between modern and warm, creating a welcoming atmosphere. Highlights include the beef short rib, bao buns and the perfectly prepared Peking duck. Altogether, a refined yet unpretentious dining experience.
reviews.author: Mads Engelbrecht

reviews.text: Great food good service!!!
reviews.author: Xavier De chalain

reviews.text: I had a pleasant experience with family at this Asian fusion restaurant in Cape Town. Had a variety of dishes to do tapas and drinks. Enjoyed the Waygu beef, the calamari and zucchini fries, beef sliders and ribs The dessert to top it off was delicious. Sorry I didn’t take many pictures of the food. I was busy enjoying it!
reviews.author: Rumbidzai Dombo

reviews.text: Lovely japanese chic ambience and fantastic bao buns and tuna dishes ☺️ very fresh and artistic
reviews.author: Hanna

reviews.text: The best Asian dishes
reviews.author: Godwin Sepeng

reviews.text:
Horrible never end up eating
Waited 20 minutes and still nobody seen to me !!! Worst experience ever!!
reviews.author: Engen Pomona

reviews.text: "Out of the box" service. Something different.
reviews.author: Johan de Kock

reviews.text: Очень понравился ресторан. Прекрасный интерьер, уютная камерная атмосфера и очень вкусная утка по-пекински
reviews.author: Alina Vavilova

reviews.text:
Upscale Asian restaurant on the Onyx Hotel.

The Sushi, Soup, and Bao Buns were excellent. Menu has interesting options. The host suggested items to try and turned out well.

The service is friendly and courteous and decor is pleasing.

Good Asian restaurant near V&A waterfront at Foreshore.
reviews.author: Manesh Modi

reviews.text: Bad cooking. Very bad. Overcooked, no autentic flavor, too creamy, lots of sugar. Etc
reviews.author: Hans Overbeek

reviews.text: Für Kapstadt, ein sehr geiles Lokal mit sehr gutem Essen
reviews.author: Michael Klotz

reviews.text: Legit great food and a large variety of Asian cuisine, enthusiastic service, acoustics are good though it wasn't busy when we were there.
reviews.author: Glen Loxton

reviews.text: Quality service, amazing food, just inside can be a bit noisy when it is crowded.
reviews.author: Ali KIZIL

reviews.text: Best place for Asian tapas, good size that accommodates every bite
reviews.author: riley amanda chapman

reviews.text: YU delivers a sophisticated and contemporary interpretation of Asian dining right in the heart of Cape Town’s vibrant Foreshore district. It seamlessly blends innovation with tradition, offering a fresh perspective on Japanese-inspired cuisine through its distinctive Izakaya-style dishes. The menu is thoughtfully designed for sharing, encouraging guests to explore a wide variety of flavors and textures—from delicate dim sum and beautifully crafted sushi to an array of imaginative Asian tapas. Each plate reflects a careful balance of creativity and authenticity, making the dining experience both exciting and deeply satisfying. Stepping into YU feels like entering an intimate, modern sanctuary. The restaurant’s dimly lit interiors, luxurious dark velvets, and warm timber accents create an atmosphere that is both elegant and inviting. Whether for a casual evening with friends, a special occasion, or a refined culinary adventure, YU offers a memorable experience that pushes the boundaries of what contemporary Asian dining can be. Fully recommended.
reviews.author: Philippe Jacques Kradolfer

reviews.text: Excellent food and very good service. A restaurant in the middle of Cape Town, adjacent to the Onyx Hotel at Heerengracht. The flavours of the food were delicious, the quality of the meat and other ingredients was superb. The waitress was friendly and attentive. I can highly recommend this place.
reviews.author: Silvia Raninger

reviews.text: What a great place to finally get to! Embarrassing that it took so long. We had three starters - lamb buns, chicken and ginger fried potstickers and traditional salmon roses. Without exception they were all outstanding - delicious and well flavoured. I note the “traditional” because they do have their own twist but will make the traditional if requested. We use these as a benchmark in Asian restaurants and these guys really passed the test. We shared the Peking duck - served at room temp - nice thin crisp skin edge to each slice and pink and juicy for the meat. Accompanied by a well balanced citrus drizzle served separately with another bowl of hoisin sauce. We enjoyed a bottle of Catherine Marshall Pinot Noir with this. Desserts described on another menu - perfect chocolate lava cake with honeycomb ice cream and a deep fried bao bun with vanilla ice cream. Shared both. A bottle of sparkling water and an espresso took this to R1645 with well deserved good tip. Very simple but effective décor. Friendly and knowledgeable service with each waiter intelligently watching their colleagues’ backs and being quietly supportive. Will be back.
reviews.author: Hilary Dennis

reviews.text:
Great food and vibe
Quality, top notched
Needed to improvise to make a reservation for a big group (20 plus people)
reviews.author: Fayaaz B

reviews.text: Great Sushi and sides
reviews.author: Tim

reviews.text: Excellent, serene and exclusive
reviews.author: Gabriel Gwinyai Gwatidzo

reviews.text: Dinner tonight. There was a bug on my table. Food was ok. I had eaten better sashimi even if the tuna was good. Then the duck was tasteless and cold and not crispy. When I finished my glass of wine, I saw 2 bugs inside my glass!!! I called the waiter and showed that to him. While doing that another cocktoach was stuck to my table!!!! He went to call the manager. He came... to the the table closed to mine... Avoiding me. I was sent... a candle and explained it is the bug season! Season of non hygiene? I paid my bill and left. I do not often write review. I am too educated to make sandal or to embarass other guests. But that worst experience ever needed to be shared. Coming from Paris my culinary travel to Capetown was so perfect until Yu. Significant inspection, desifectisation and guest management is  required.
reviews.author: Carole Libock

reviews.text: YU sours 110%
reviews.author: Jan “Rolo” Van Schalkwyk

reviews.text:
What an experience. The food is pricey but it is delicious. We had an allergy at our table and they dealt with it well. Tapas style food was perfect for the picky eaters. They brought the good out as it was ready so we were already eating.

The veg bao bun was something I even knew existed but it was delicious. The meat eaters said the sliders were the best.

The sushi was clean cut. Veg sushi was interesting. Not the best mix of flavour but I enjoyed it. Edamame beans was average. Note the deep fried sushi is not all deep fried, just the outside. They still enjoyed it though.

Best dish was the the veg and short rib Bao buns. We didn't have from the plates.

The service was good even though it was busy.

I didn't check if they had WiFi but it's in a hotel so I figure they do.

Ambiance was good. When it got full, it got quite loud though.

Place of clean. Food is expensive. Experience was amazing. Going back of I can.
reviews.author: Nkateko Mathebula

reviews.text: Really nice place if you're looking for a mix of Asian meals! Service was sooo friendly and really went an extra mile for us. Some small comments, it is quite loud at peak hours, food is on the pricier side and could do with a bit of salt but overall it was a really good experience! Thanks!
reviews.author: Felix K

reviews.text: The food is Unbelievably tasty . Loved every single dish .
reviews.author: mishkaat Wagner

reviews.text: The food at Yu is amazingly delicious! Very tasty and reasonably priced.
reviews.author: Phelokazi

reviews.text: Went there today and it wasn’t too busy. Intimate space with attentive waiters and reasonably priced food. Good for sharing as they specialise in tapas
reviews.author: Precious Nondanyana

reviews.text: Not the cheapest , but excellent tasty food
reviews.author: Helga de Vasconcelos Sarria

reviews.text: Seems Yu is no longer an undiscovered gem. Excellent food in a pleasant ambience. Used to be our go-to pre-show  pop-in - but worthy of a much more lingering treat...
reviews.author: Charley Lewis

reviews.text: Delicious food, excellent service and great location. The acoustics are very poor though - the only reason they last a star is because we were a group of 6 and could only chat to the person right next to us and not with the whole table.
reviews.author: Bronwen Aspeling de Villiers

reviews.text: Heaven on various plates!
reviews.author: Melissa Smit

reviews.text: I'm writing to provide some feedback on my recent visit. While I enjoyed the overall experience, I was a bit disappointed with the prawn curry. The prawns themselves seemed to lack flavor, and I felt the portion size was a bit small. Additionally, the service was quite slow from the moment I ordered my drink and meal, even though the restaurant didn't seem very busy.
reviews.author: Smash Afrika

reviews.text: The restaurant was an amazing experience form the friendly and helpful service to the delicious foods. Waiting for a table does not take long especially when you book a table beforehand. The cuisines are filling and full of flavour even though the portions appeared small. The noise level from the music is low however the noise level mainly comes from your neighbouring tables. Despite the noise level you can still have a conversation without screaming. The service was very well coming and helpful in suggesting dishes. The atmosphere of the restaurant is lovely and appears hidden which is suiting as I believe I have found a hidden gem.
reviews.author: Oti Kekana

reviews.text: Beautiful interior, friendly staff, tasty asian food, but! too pricey for locals.. you need several plated not to be hungry 😅 one time experience
reviews.author: Olga Bykovs

reviews.text: Great food! We love it!
reviews.author: Tasneem Kamaloodien

reviews.text:
Good lunch at Yu. After 6 years back and still great asian flavors and creative dishes. We had 6 dishes to share which was enough but not too much either. Especially the beef short rib bao bun, beef sliders, aubergine and tuna sashimi were really good. Sticky ribs also nice and tender, came of the bone immediately. The chicken karaage was a bit difficult to decide if I really tasted chicken or that it was just a lot of fried batter and a spicy sauce.

The ambience if good but only 2 tables on a Sunday afternoon (which was also the case 6 years ago) is a bit of a damper on the vibe).

Prices also reasonable for Cape Town CBD. Staff was friendly.
reviews.author: Astrid Witteveen

reviews.text: Not bad, but duck pancakes will disappoint you.
reviews.author: Agne Rosensverd

reviews.text: Just had a fantastic meal at YU. The food was amazing and delicious. The restaurant was lovely and had a great atmosphere and design. The waiters were just right - helpful and polite. I also had a great cocktail, the Coconut express 👍
reviews.author: Tara Higgs

reviews.text: Amazing food. Very good service. Will definitely be back.
reviews.author: Neil Dreyersmith

reviews.text: Everything was perfect! The food, every item we ordered, was delicious. The Saigo pudding was divine.
reviews.author: Sue Pillay

reviews.text: This is pur favorite place for all purposes team lunches.
reviews.author: Denise Barnard

reviews.text: Can get a bit too busy over the weekend. 4th floor accommodates same restaurant with city views and a more sophisticated space for quiet moments. Food is amazing.
reviews.author: Nonofo Nkwe

reviews.text: Top-notch Cape Town Dining
reviews.author: Max Gansel

reviews.text:
This was probably one of the best meals I have had in my life, hands down👌👏. The waiters are amazing. The food is on another level and the service was just impeccable. Thank you to YU restaurant for setting the standard on how service should be delivered.
They stole my heart when they brought me a toothpick after I've had some ribs. I don't know how many times I've been to a place that serves steak and meat, but don't think about a simple thing like a toothpick. Once again. Thank you for an incredible experience 👏👍. 5 stars all the way!
reviews.author: Gershwyn Spannenberg

reviews.text: Asian Tapas. You'll enjoy it.
reviews.author: Saberi Marais

reviews.text: Prawn sandwiches tasted like they left the rice out for a day but the rest of the food was amazing! The best service I’ve ever received.
reviews.author: Thabang Makawu

reviews.text: Food mediocre at best. Service excellent. I think ideally stick to the tapas and not the main dishes.
reviews.author: Xavier Ariel R

reviews.text: I enjoyed their food and service
reviews.author: Maphala Mamaregane

reviews.text: Excellent service, exquisite food, generous portions ( beware of the ribs), a bit austere surroundings but overall excellent and fairly priced as well. Go back anytime!
reviews.author: Beauty Ikudehinbu

reviews.text: Amazing food and service
reviews.author: crystal twaddle

reviews.text: Great food and service!
reviews.author: Gini Desai

reviews.text: We were there at lunchtime. Food was outstanding. Probably more atmospheric in the evening. Struggled to find the entrance to the parking for the Onyx Hotel, ended up driving round a couple of times!
reviews.author: Debbie Hayes

reviews.text: Wonderful place right across the street from CTICC. Various asian lunch options in a relaxed atmosphere with great service.
reviews.author: Grigore Stamatescu

reviews.text: Entre al restaurante pero la música estaba muy alta. Opte por ir a otro lugar
reviews.author: Rodrigo Wayland

reviews.text: Good food and very good service for a quick lunch
reviews.author: Mike Silber

reviews.text:
Really decent little restaurant with a good selection of Asian food. When there’s a big conference on at the nearby CTICC service can be terribly slow. Just be warned.
Great food though.
reviews.author: Mark

reviews.text:
Yu Restaurant is incredible! Our waiter Thobile was fantastic - very friendly and attentive, often checking we were ok and that we were enjoying the food. He was able to give us great recommendations for both food and drink.
We ordered a few dishes including sushi, dim sum, bao buns, and red coconut chicken and beef stir fry for mains. All were beautifully cooked and tasted delicious. The sushi in particular was lovely, especially the beef sushi special!
The atmosphere was calm and relaxed. We are staying in the Onyx Hotel and may just eat here every night!
Highly recommend.
reviews.author: Leanne Cadman

reviews.text: Very friendly. I mean really. They care about food. Our food ambassador knew his menu. He made excellent recommendations. Sadly didn’t mange to get photos of everything.
reviews.author: Simon Bradford

reviews.text: Had such a wonderful experience dining here. All of the food was delicious, especially the chocolate dessert. Would definitely visit again and highly highly recommend.
reviews.author: Kayla Cohen

reviews.text:
Probably my new favorite place in CPT. If you truly value GOOD FOOD you need to go there. The food quality is insane and the Menue has so many amazing options.
Everyone is super nice and professional.
reviews.author: franzi lou

reviews.text:
Probably the most underrated place in Cape Town due to its location.

Absolutely fantastic Japanese food and super friendly staff.
reviews.author: Thomas Knappitsch

reviews.text: Been a consistent favourite over the years. Truly exceptional flavours. Of the best Cape Town has to offer!
reviews.author: Luca Bester

reviews.text: Love the ambiance of this place. It also helps that the food is amazing. We especially love to have nibbles and tapas from the menu and share to have variety. Katsu chicken bao, beef short rib bao, prawn rock shrimp bao, wagyu sliders are our favorites. It can get loud on weekend nights. So if you are planning a catch up with a close friend opt for a late afternoon instead.
reviews.author: wardah botha

reviews.text: Amazing food, awesome/friendly staff and I the centre of everything, recommended for sure 👌
reviews.author: Tristan Machard

reviews.text: Awesome atmosphere. Great options for food, very different and good. We were a large table of 22 and they handled us quite well. Food just took a little long sometimes but still very good. The bao buns were very yum and the spicy crispy rice was very different and delicious. Good experience for us and I would recommend for sure.
reviews.author: Malcolm Wright

reviews.text:
We had a truly wonderful dinner here. The ambience, service, hospitality, food, and drinks were outstanding as a whole! Would love to come back and sample more of their menu. The rock prawn bao buns were incredible! You know it's good when even the basic California rolls are the best you've ever had. The Indonesian stir-fry and Sago dessert were also delicious.

Only disappointment was the Pekin duck main dish being too dry, so just okay.
reviews.author: Shalyn Jordaan

reviews.text: Visited as a group of 5, it had been recommended by others in our hockey team who had visited previously. Friendly welcome from staff. A luvly bright, clean, spacious restaurant. We ordered a variety of food, and all our food was delicious, I ordered Peking Duck, which came beautifully presented as did all our dishes. Sweets were also njoyed as was my coffee. It's a luvly restaurant, a really nice atmosphere. Worth a visit :) :)
reviews.author: Anne Harvey-chisholm

reviews.text: It was one of the best meals I had in a long time. Everything was delicious! Highly recommend the ribeye steak with fried rice. Absolutely delicious. The service was excellent as well. I will definitely try more items on the menu!
reviews.author: Zelda van Staden

reviews.text: I really enjoyed the salmon poke bowl I had here, there was a generous amount in f fish and it had lettuce in which I prefer. The drinks were okay but sometimes the flavours were a bit overpowering. The decor is stunning, it makes a good date spot
reviews.author: Mugisha Hakizimana

reviews.text: I really love the vibe and atmosphere of Yu.  The concierge at Westin recommended it to us and made sure we were taken care of.  Yu staff greeted us with warm smiles and we received exceptional service from all.  My husband ordered a cucumber salad and they brought out a bowl of pickles.  That was a bit different for us as we weren't expecting pickles.  Everything else ordered was delicious, down to the fried rice.  The drinks were superb as well.  We are putting this on our list to return.  I highly recommend Yu restaurant.
reviews.author: Carmelita Hanks

reviews.text:
YU restaurant is located within the Onyx hotel which is directly opposite the CTICC. So this is a great spot if you're looking for lunch or dinner after your event at the CTICC.

The menu is Asian fusion style dishes. I ordered the chicken and ginger fried dimsum, egg fried rice, grilled broccoli and the sticky ribs. The food is tasty but is perhaps slightly too complicated. Just keep that in mind if you're looking for a traditional Asian restaurant, this is not that.

The service is impeccable and the restaurant is beautifully decorated.
reviews.author: Prinesh Reddy

reviews.text: The food was excellent and  the service was amazing. With so many options to choose from, the waitress gave us suggestions which were absolutely delicious. The tapas was a hit with our group. Our vegetarian friend had so many options as well, which is always a bonus.  Will definitely be going back to sample the rest of the menu
reviews.author: Mandy Naidoo

reviews.text:
First, the interior shows off environmental features & sustainability in a welcoming manner, the kinoe-looking chairs speak to preserving ‘derelics’ or useless waste into something useable; the walls are adorned with some Japanese matchbox art that kind of gives you snapshots of what art, advertising, culture was in the 20th century.
Second, Precious is GOLD & amazing!
Third, never in my life have I ever tasted such perfectly smoked chicken (wings) — finding the right balance to not burn the chicken, preserve the smoked flavour & add a hint of spice w/out overpowering my tastebuds is 1 helluva skill🤯! The meat was simply falling off the bones, OMG! And, guys, that wasn’t even my favourite meal, the prawn rock shrimp almost brought tears to my eyes, the bun was so fluffy & warm, items were having a flavour-bomb party in my mouth, I just—…!
reviews.author: Tash N.

reviews.text:
I visited the restaurant because I was renting a flat at ONEX hotel.
The menu was big but I tried my best guess to get delicious dishes.
I really liked seafood soup as it was extraordinarily.
Chicken was undercooked, duck was average but sauce was good.

What I like is when the waitress arranged a sweet for me when she knew that I was at my honeymoon time.
The food was a bit pricey.
reviews.author: Rayed AlMalki

reviews.text: A great dining experience. We enjoyed our seared, crusted tuna, though we would have liked to see all sides of the fish crusted as well as a better cut of fish. The bao buns were alright as well, definitely better than many other places, but still nothing too incredible. One of our party ordered the salmon and very much liked the sauce and the cut of fish. Biggest complaint is that there were way too many specials for an evening - keeping it to just 2-3 specials that the chef could do well would result in superior specials.
reviews.author: Dax Rich

reviews.text: Just amazing! I cannot wait to visit again, as there are some dishes I want to try. The fried ginger chicken dumplings and sticky ribs were delicious! Sesame crusted tuna was faultless. Atmosphere and service was great!
reviews.author: Shahieda Ebrahim

reviews.text:
interesting modern asian fusion food located inside onyx hotel. loved the ambience inside. service was great and staffs were very friendly.
food was not bad. loved the lamb chop and wagyu sliders. their sushi was decent too.
reviews.author: Jutika Krongmongkolkul

reviews.text: We had the best experience ever! The staff were friendly and warm. The food was rich in flavour and textures. The Poké bowl should’ve had a little more rice, but overall the experience was worthwhile despite it being a bit on the pricey side. I thoroughly enjoyed the chicken and chive Dim Sum as well. I’d highly recommend this place to anyone who loves Japanese food as there is quite a variety to choose from!
reviews.author: Amy October

reviews.text:
Has great potential but misses the mark on most dishes. Was lacking in the balanced flavours that Asian cuisine requires but since the flavour profiles of the core seasonings are already good it is a satisfactory dining experience.
Skip the dessert - not worth it.
reviews.author: Nesh Mungroo

reviews.text: Super humbled by the experience here. Welcoming waiters, fast service, beautiful ambience for dinner. The manager made it extra special by making sure we were looked after exceptionally well... even treated us to complimentary desert! Great energy and humour. Every dish was served perfectly and did not dissapoint. Vast variety to choose from and vegeterian friendly. You can feel the passion put into this place, it's a hidden gem in the city. The best fine dining service we've had in Capetown! Thank you Yu team!
reviews.author: angelique wood

reviews.text: Yu has been on my radar for ages, but admittedly, the location did deter me for a while, but on Friday after a networking event, we headed into town. We called ahead to ensure they could accommodate us. Upon arrival, the restaurant felt moody and modern. The only negative and its really not their fault is the bright light from the Media24 digital billboard, which is like a torch in your face. Nonetheless, the service was wonderful and welcoming. Our waitress gave us some insight as to how to order and her suggestions, which we followed. We ordered the Chicken and Ginger potstickers, Chicken and Sriracha Bao Buns, Wagyu Beef Tetaki, and the Peking Duck with pancakes. Everything was tasty and delicious 😋.  Potstickers fried, super crispy with a wonderful hum of ginger. The bao is soft and pillowy, the chicken filling crispy. The pancakes were thin and soft with Duck that was perfectly cooked (sorry, forgot to take pics). As there was so much food, we didn't have space for dessert 😔 but we have vowed to return, very soon!
reviews.author: Meegan Kieffer

reviews.text: Amazing food and staff. Nothing less than 5 stars. We had several different items to eat, from calamari, ribs, bar buns, prawns, zuchinni fries, butternut mussel soup, edamamme beans, it was all incredible. Fantastic food, great cocktails and always amazing. Yu, you never disappoint, thank you. The only thing I would ask for is candles on the tables to be able to read the menu.
reviews.author: Kim Sharba

reviews.text: I simply cannot keep my eyes open whilst eating the food here! From the time we enter the place till the time we leave we are extremely well taken care of. Hats off to the entire team for giving us all a beautiful dining experience ans the most mouthwatering food. Compliments to you all! We will definitely make this a regular stop whenever we are in the Cape!
reviews.author: Sashnam Sunnylall

reviews.text:
Amazing Asian twist resturant. We had several starters/tapas/bites. The portions were generous and the quality and flavour was exceptional.
Highly recommended!
reviews.author: Gideon van T

reviews.text: Had the most lovely experience! Restaurant is clean & beautiful, service was outstanding. Food is ABSOLUTELY out of this world. Pricey - but worth it.
reviews.author: Stephani le Roux

reviews.text:
Friday afternoon, lunch at YU at the Onyx Hotel. To start with: the food was amazing, really really good! I’m a big fan of asian food and this place nails it.
I did some research before we went, I selected some of the dishes that were recommended and that I wanted to try myself. We eventually made a choice of 6 dishes and 2 sides + 1 dessert.
The portions are big, they call it ‘snacks’ but it’s so much more than that. We had the edamame beans, soft shell crab, sticky ribs, tuna seared salad, fried wagyu dim sum, duck bao bun, aubergine side dish, seafood fried rice and the chocolate fondant as dessert (to share because we we’re already very satisfied at that time).
The wines were also very good, we had a Chenin Blanc, Cabernet and Syrah.
It was well worth the money. Did I already say it was really really good?
I’m sure we’ll be back to try more of the menu (or just the same, also pretty fine with me ;-)
reviews.author: Astrid Witteveen

reviews.text: A gem in the CBD of Cape Town, excellent service, delicious sushi / Asian cuisine , a feast of eclectic starters , some with a twist. I'll definitely be going there again. A perfect stop for dinner gefire going to the Theater.
reviews.author: Charles Pretorius

reviews.text: The food at the restaurant was absolutely out of this world, I can't fault anything and enjoyed every single dish we had! Unfortunately the service was a bit too overwhelming. We were checked on too often and kept having to stop out conversations and the instant we finished a plate and still chewing out plates were cleared. It can become a bit frustrating when it's a bit invasive. The venue and ambience was still fantastic nonetheless
reviews.author: Cindy Joao

reviews.text: Fine dinning restaurant. Expect to spend at least R200. I suggest getting different tapas instead of one main meal. Food is delicious. Portions are a bit small. Service is fast and friendly. Table lighting is great.
reviews.author: Adam Mukuddem

reviews.text: The absolute best of the best. Asian fusion style restaurant situated in a hotel called the onyx in the CBD which makes it a destination type restaurant, and it is always busy which just shows how good it is. It attracts a loyal customer base. The staff are all friendly, well presented and know the menu very well. Most of them have been here for years, and they make a point to remember their loyal customers from year to year. I went with two friends and we ordered a variety of dishes , every single one was 10/10. Edamame beans, chicken wings, dim sum is the best you can get, sushi, short rib bao buns unreal. Our waitress Precious was amazing. Also the food comes very quickly. It is great to order a lot of small dishes and to share. Thanks to Yu for another amazing meal and I look forward to visiting again soon!
reviews.author: Mark Stillerman

reviews.text:
Absolutely amazing!!! I’d give a 5, however, they don’t have a written dessert menu, so the waiter tells you, and misunderstandings can happen, as it occurred to us (hard to understand his English accent).
He said they had a green tea fondant, when it came, it was a normal chocolate fondant, so we were a bit disappointed. Other than that: really good menu, interesting mix of flavors.
reviews.author: Ana Wen de Oliveira

reviews.text: The food was fast, fresh and delicious. Ask for the off menu fried chicken and ginger dumplings, we also had the lamb chops, prawn bar and Thai papaya salad, all were great
reviews.author: Bhavna Maharaj Naidoo

reviews.text:
We tried Yu today and staff were friendly and attentive. The food was really good. I would definitely recommend the chicken wings, chicken and sriracha bao and the chicken fried dumplings! Those were our favorites. Everything was good aside from the seafood steamed dim-sum, it was a bit fishy for our taste!

Will definitely be back! There’s so much more in the menu to try
reviews.author: Chantal Naidoo

reviews.text: Love coming to Yu! Our fave spot for date night and I recently brought my parents here for a dinner out, lovely staff, food is great and love the decor! Cocktails are great, highly recommend the Wagyu sliders to start and I always get the poke bowl!!
reviews.author: Sian

reviews.text: I enjoyed my visit here. 5star service with a knowledgeable waitor who offered us excellent options based on our preferences. He was able to educate us about the dishes on the menu, assist me with choosing the wine I liked and the food was delicious, filling and well presented. We waited a little longer than we had expected to between our starters and mains because we were very hungry but the food was well worth the wait. The table was a little dark though as it was located between down lights. We had to use our phone torches to see what we were served better. A candle or some sort of soft light on the table wud be appreciated. Yu, I'm looking forward to visiting You again 😃
reviews.author: Kazi Kawa

reviews.text: Exceptional dinner. Calamari appetizer was out of this world good. Incredible sushi. Great Peking duck.  Phenomenal meal all around. Perseverance and Prince were outstanding waiters. Attentive& engaging but not over-bearing.
reviews.author: tigerstyle

reviews.text:
What an amazing meal
We shared the meals between two of us.
We ordered the chicken money bagd deep fried dim sum. This was the best.
We also had the chicken boa bun which was delicious.
And then we shared the Peking duck with pancakes which was incredible.

Cheesecake and ginger crumble for dessert.

The atmosphere was great and good selection of wine as well.

The staff were really friendly.
reviews.author: Heloise Steyn

reviews.text:
Visited Yu for the first time and had a lovely meal. We also had a recommended special - cauliflower bao buns, which were packed with flavour.
The atmosphere was vibey, good lighting and interior decor. The service and attention to detail was excellent! Definrely recommend!
reviews.author: Candice Schroeder

reviews.text: They have the best Tapas in the city and their Sushi is better than so many of the so called Sushi places in Cape Town . They also have a great selection for vegans.
reviews.author: Khanyisane Falake

reviews.text: An absolutely amazing experience. Food is really good. Flavour adventure. Our waiter, Charlie, is an incredible human. Thank you for an amazing birthday.
reviews.author: Muhammad Manuel

reviews.text: Food is good. Limited options but it's prepared very well. Special mention for the Lamb chops. However the prices are quite high. Not exactly an Asian restaurant. Seems more like fusion.
reviews.author: Rick R

reviews.text: Recommended for an early dinner before our show at Artscape (a quick 3 min drive to the theatre, free parking available in front of the restaurant which is in The Onyx). Good portions and great flavour. We chose some tapas, fried chicken and ginger potstickers, short rib bao bun, Asian mushroom bao bun, chicken skewers and hoisin calamari. Delish! Service from Joe was great too. Overall a
reviews.author: Ashleigh Ingram

reviews.text: HAIR IN YOUR FOOD. SUCKS. We went to order sushi and when we placed our order. Our waiter advised us that they're full and they're going to take really long and we should consider something else. Almost as if he didn't want us to order it. We didn't mind waiting so we ordered. Then I got my food and there were 2 pieces of hair sticking out of it. Sent it back and we have full view of the sushi bar and they pulled out the hair and it was right through like a 30cm hair. It was disgusting. He offered to make it again and mind you my whole table got their food already now I must wait more. Did nothing to compensate for their mistake. Wanna be all fancy but service and food sucks. Overpriced and over rated.
reviews.author: Mogammad Faisal Baulackey

reviews.text:
Honestly this used to be my favorite restaurant. I took an international guest with my for lunch and it was a failure. The dim sum was tiny. So tiny that my guest thought i brought him to this restaurant as an example of a”place bot to visit”. The sushi was awful and we eventually cancelled the rest of the meal. The manager usually comes to every table but this time he sat nearby and didn’t bother. We asked for him and still he didn’t come over. He even went to cancel our dishes and nothing. The waiter didn’t even come back to let us know our dishes had in fact been cancelled, she just left us for a while. Only after trying to get her attention and asking her about the status, she said “oh yeah, it’s canceled”. I’ll get your bill.

Anyway, a much loved favourite will not longer being seeing us. Not cool.
reviews.author: Aerin Rain

reviews.text: Please avoid dining in this restaurant at all cost. Not only the way for the food took nearly 1 hour when there were only 2 tables including ours there but the service from server Joe was extremely rude. If it wasn’t for my family I would have made a scene there. This is my first negative google review after traveling around the world and eating at all types of restaurants simply because of how bad the service of the staff is. You don’t throw the receipt to your customer. The management that is reading this, you all should be ashamed for the serving the worst standards of Asian fusion food (the Peking duck was dry as a rubber and the fried rice was flat), but more critically due to the rude service from Joe, this puts the African hospitality and service levels to shame. I will make sure to share my first worst dining experience in CapeTown, South Africa with everyone I know to avoid this restaurant. Just shutdown the damn business if you guys don’t have any pride in service levels, or to serve basic minimum quality food. We don’t owe you a living, if no customers, there is no business. I am not sure even if this was racist simply because we are asians and not whites?
reviews.author: Mark Leong

reviews.text: After 1.5 hours we received our food only after we ask the status.   We were the second group inside the restaurant so our order should have been quickly processed.  The lamb was sent back,  the tuna just as bad.   The whole experience was terrible.  Star rating is too high but fractional parts are not shown.
reviews.author: Eddie Gruben

reviews.text: We waited almost 2u hours to get are food by uber and never got delivered. We ordered at 19u30 and after more than 10calls to the restaurant and the hotel, the had now answer and no food to send us. The poor uber delivery boy had waited also for a long time. This is no service at all!!! Big disappointment and we go never again there!!!
reviews.author: Erns Wout

reviews.text:
The server who took my order was great, but the food was shocking. I ordered the Thai papaya salad and rib bo buns with a cheese cake to go. The papaya salad was inedible. I've made that dish myself, the dressing is a combination of lime, fish sauce and a few other light items. This dressing was thick, dominated the dish, was way too much, and I could taste the preservatives, straight from a jar or can. The salad itself was woody and flavorless. Big strips of cabbage that I could barely chew I couldn't eat much. The waiter asked me if I wasnt hungry and I said no the salad was terrible... But she turned and walked away before I even finished the sentence.

The bo bun was heavy, all BBQ sauce and not much else. It was filling but really nothing special. The cheese cake was actually sour and was starting to go off. It was probably over a week old and even the consistency was awful. I thought I was going to get sick after a few bites, as I had a few stomach pains after this meal.

The food is really bad, the restaurant needs a cleaning...stains on the seat... Cannot recommend in a town with so many wonderful choices.
reviews.author: Jesse Peery

reviews.text: Everything was good at the moment of the meal, but in the next day I got food poisoning. Once I’ve not eaten anything else from the meal at Yu to the point of starting to vomit and diarrhea, I’m inclined to believe it was something at Yu. Probably the salmon or the eel.
reviews.author: Rodrigo Benclowicz

reviews.text:
This is NOT an authentic Japanese or Asian restaurant.
Dishes are westernised and over flavoured to make up for the "conveyer-belt" factory style food that's churned out of the kitchen. Out of 7 dishes ordered I can onlt partially compliment 1 of them. Extremely disappointed.
reviews.author: jovana harkhu

reviews.text: I have visited and eaten at this restaurant many times this time was terrible the portions smaller , the fillet was cold , guys your prices are steep so your food has to be 100 .% not at all pleased with this experience
reviews.author: Issy Levy

reviews.text: Very disapointing. we were staying at the hotel and did room service. the food was burnt and tasteless. so disgusting that we did not eat it. such a waste.
reviews.author: Reve d'enfant

reviews.text: Service is good but food is a big NO NO NO NO NO NOOOOO i was literally about to barf all over them
reviews.author: Modi Alareifi

reviews.text: Absolute rip off. Over priced R95 for a beer in a can. Food 2/10
reviews.author: Mark Hall

reviews.text: Highly disappointed. Rubber seafood in tomato soup. For R110!!??
reviews.author: Brandon Carolus

reviews.text: Too expensive for the actual experience
reviews.author: Nubia Manjezi

reviews.text: Long wait everyday 🤦
reviews.author: Oswald Harerimana
